# Gartner® Emerging Tech: The Future of Online Fraud Prevention

![pettern](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20684%20460'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Is your business prepared for how online fraud prevention will change by the end of the decade? Gartner has released its **Emerging Tech: The Future of Online Fraud Prevention** report to help risk management leaders navigate the convergence of fraud prevention, identity, and cybersecurity.

Key projections from the report include:

*   “Cyber-fraud fusion is expected to accelerate and become increasingly important through 2031 as organizations of all sizes continue to reach the understanding that online fraud is a cybersecurity issue.”
*   “Cyber-fraud fusion describes an emerging trend within the security market where elements of cybersecurity, identity and online fraud prevention are combined into holistic defenses to combat fraud online.” 
*   “The shift from online fraud prevention to cyber-fraud fusion will occur in phases over the next six years. However, organizations will approach each stage differently depending upon their industry, size and level of maturity.”

According to Gartner, “[…] at least 50% of large financial institutions and online retailers will consolidate online fraud prevention personnel, duties and responsibilities into cybersecurity teams reporting to the CISO.”*

Get complimentary access to the **Emerging Tech: The Future of Online Fraud Prevention** report from Gartner for insights on integrated, intelligent, and identity-focused protection.

*Gartner, Emerging Tech: The Future of Online Fraud Prevention, Dan Ayoub, Pete Redshaw, Sean ONeill, Vatsal Sharma, 30 January 2025  

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and is used herein with permission. All rights reserved.
